titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in China ( (Stuart) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in China ( Stuart ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1595. Following is text note received today by Director Nanking USISJosiah W. Bennett, Assistant Attaché, Director of United States Information Service. from MCC.Military Control Commission.

quote.p:
“As the United States Information Service is a part of the United States Department of State organization, and since the People’s Government has no diplomatic or consular relations with the United States, the United States Information Service must cease its activities as from today. Such activities include the printing and distribution of news bulletins, the exhibition and presentation of books and papers, exhibition of illustrated posters, movie showings, holding of musical
concerts and all other public activities. It is hoped that you will comply without fail.”The Ambassador in China stated in his telegram No. 1589, July 23, not printed: “USIS Nanking today discontinued public activities as result notification from Nanking Alien Affairs Bureau.” (124.936/7–2349)

div.p: Sent Department 1595, OffEmb Canton 668, ConGen Shanghai 893, Hankow 56, Peiping 290, Tientsin 62, ConGen Canton 40.
